questions.question: Explain multiple inheritance in C++ with an example syntax.
keyPoints: Multiple inheritance allows a derived class to inherit from more than one base class
Syntax uses comma-separated list of base classes with access specifiers
Example: class Teacher : public Person, public Employee {}

questions.question: What is the Diamond Problem? How is it resolved in C++?
keyPoints: Diamond problem occurs when a class inherits from two classes that share a common base
Creates ambiguity about which path to use for inherited members
Resolved using virtual inheritance (virtual keyword)
Ensures only one copy of common base class exists

questions.question: Explain the constructor and destructor call order in multiple inheritance.
keyPoints: Base class constructors called in order they appear in inheritance list
Derived class constructor executes after all base constructors
Destructors called in reverse order of constructors
Derived class destructor runs first, then base class destructors in reverse order

questions.question: How do you resolve ambiguity when base classes have members with same name?
keyPoints: Use scope resolution operator (::) to specify which base class member
Syntax: object.BaseClass::memberName
Can also use 'using' declaration to bring specific members into scope
May require explicit qualification to avoid ambiguity

questions.question: What is virtual inheritance? Write the syntax for it.
keyPoints: Virtual inheritance ensures single copy of base class in inheritance hierarchy
Used to solve diamond problem
Syntax: class Derived : virtual public Base {}
Virtual base is initialized by the most derived class

questions.question: Design a C++ program demonstrating multiple inheritance with classes Person (name, age), Employee (emp_id, salary), and a derived class Manager that inherits from both Person and Employee. Include appropriate constructors and display functions.
keyPoints: Define Person class with name, age, constructor and display method
Define Employee class with emp_id, salary, constructor and display method
Define Manager class inheriting from both Person and Employee using public inheritance
Manager should have additional attributes like department
Show constructor calling order and demonstrate ambiguity resolution if needed
Include proper access specifiers and demonstrate usage

questions.question: Explain the Diamond Problem in detail with a diagram. Write a C++ program showing how virtual inheritance solves this problem. Include constructor order observations.
keyPoints: Explain diamond structure: Class D inherits from B and C, both inherit from A
Without virtual inheritance: two copies of A exist in D, causing ambiguity
With virtual inheritance: only one copy of A exists in D
Show correct syntax with virtual keyword
Demonstrate that B and C don't initialize A directly
Most derived class (D) initializes the virtual base class A
Show constructor order: virtual base (A) first, then non-virtual bases (B, C), then derived (D)

questions.question: Write a C++ program that demonstrates constructor invocation order in multiple inheritance. Also show how to explicitly call base class constructors with arguments in a derived class.
keyPoints: Create multiple base classes with parameterized constructors
Derived class uses initializer list to call base constructors with arguments
Print statements to show constructor calling order
Demonstrate destructor calling order (reverse of constructors)
Show that arguments can be passed to any base class constructor
Demonstrate error cases if base constructor not called explicitly (default needed)
